title: [Winlogbeat] 8.x Module pipelines not setting event.module
body: In 8.x the Winlogbeat modules moved to an Ingest Node implementation based on Fleet pipelines. But in Fleet a constant_keyword event.module definition is used so the pipeline does not set the value. In Beats we cannot use a constant_keyword since all modules write to the same index, so each module pipeline needs updated to set `event.module: {name_of_module}`.

title: Cleanup ServiceLocator for better performance
body:
NuGet does a *lot* of custom things in the ServiceLocator. 

This task tracks cleaning up the unnecessary thread switches.

The gist is: 

* Avoid using methods that access the UI thread when request services exposed via MEF. Use free threaded calls, or ideally uses MEF composition. 
* Don't use custom methods to retrieve services from the service provider. Use the built in extensibility.

# Proposal
Make a version of `ComposedParticle` that would allow children to use their own lifespan.

title: [proposal] mark classes constructors as const
body:
When writing a new class, a developer cannot mark their constructor as const if the superclass from which they are deriving doesn't have a const constructor. Certain classes in flutter are not marked as const. They should be const by default.  

The more widgets that are marked as const, the more likely performance improvements during render/re-render.

title: Beta 4: ArgumentArity.Zero required/missing
body:
Until Beta 4 I used `new Option(...)` for a "flag" option that could be present or not; simply tested for with `HasOption()`.

Since Beta 4 `Option` is abstract and requires an instantiation with a type, e.g. `new Option<bool>(...)`. This changes the parsing though! You can now still use it as a "flag" option : `--flag`, but also as `--flag=true` and (this is the confusing one): `--flag=false`.

title: Explain Fidesops Privacy Request Execution
body:

Fidesops has a certain set of steps it currently follows to access/erase data/resume failed privacy requests etc. It currently access data in a specific order and erases data in a specific order.

title: Bug: different image sizes for different templatestyles
body:
Based on the templatestyle the number of padded zeros around images changes, which changes figure size. 

Possible solution: if MNI is provided, rescale XYZ to the size of the input MNI at the end of netplotbrain.plot.

title: mapfishapp - "load data from catalog" improvement
body:
When performing a search in a catalog with mapfishapp, we currently expect the layer name + service URL to be filled in the dataset metadata.

This will not always be like this, since the organization which creates/owns the dataset and its associated metadata is not always the same as the one who manages the service to share the data.
